title: GT2010-23082
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
Components and Labels

*   Figure 1: Counter rotating coaxial radial swirlecup arrangement
*   Figure 2: Base configuration with splash plate and combustion dome attached to the exit, Configuration 1 with splash plate only
*   Figure 3: Experimental Facility Schematic
*   Figure 4: Measurement Coordinate System

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirl cup effective area: 0.62 in²
*   Dome expansion angle difference: 9 degrees
*   Chamber dimensions: 133.6 x 114.3 x 330.2 mm
*   Test section thickness: 1.6 mm
*   Manifold PVC pipe diameter: 203 mm
*   Air pipe diameter: 50 mm
*   Air pressure: up to 6.8 bars
*   Air flow rate: up to 1 kg/s
*   Seeding particle size: 1-5 µm
*   Laser focal lengths: 500 mm (transmitter), 300 mm (receiver)
*   Temperature: 70 ± 1 °F
*   Total pressure loss: 4.9%
*   Data count: >7000 (green laser), >3000 (blue laser)
*   Velocity uncertainty: 0.1-0.3 m/s
*   Horizontal measurement plane location: 5 mm from flare
*   Horizontal measurement grid resolution: 2 mm (X and Y)
*   Vertical measurement locations (Z/R): 0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.6, 2, 4, 6, 6.68, 8
*   Vertical measurement grid resolution: 1 mm (radial and tangential)
*   Width to breadth ratio of the SAC sector: 85%

**6. Nomenclature:**
*   Va = Axial velocity component
*   Vr = Radial velocity component
*   Vt = Tangential velocity component
*   RMSA = Root mean square value of the axial velocity
*   RMSR = Root mean square value of the radial velocity
*   RMST = Root mean square value of the tangential velocity
*   R = Flare exit radius
*   CFD = Computational Fluid Dynamics
*   SAC = Single Annular Combustor
*   PIV = Particle Image Velocimetry
*   LDV = Laser Doppler Velocimetry
*   GTC = Gas Turbine Combustor
*   CRZ = Central Recirculation Zone
*   DPSS = Diode Pumped Solid State
*   X, Y, Z = Cartesian coordinate system with the origin defined at the flare center

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

        *   X/R (dimensionless)
        *   Y/R (dimensionless)
    *   Units: RMSA (Root Mean Square of the Axial Velocity): m/s
    *   Curves: Axial velocity represented as contours.  Figure 7 has RMSA values ranging from 5 to 22 m/s. Figure 8 shows values from 4 to 12 m/s.

*   **Figure 9 & 10:**
    *   Axes:

        *   0.8: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -5 to +12 m/s

        *   2: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -13 to +5 m/s

        *   6: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -5 to +10 m/s
        *   6.68: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -2 to +5 m/s
        *   8: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -1 to +5 m/s
    *   Curves (Figure 10):
        *   0.2: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -13 to +22 m/s
        *   0.4: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -10 to +15 m/s
        *   0.8: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -5 to +10 m/s
        *   1.6: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -1 to +5 m/s
        *   2: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -15 to +6 m/s
        *   4: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -2 to +12 m/s
        *   6: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -5 to +17 m/s
        *   6.68: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -2 to +8 m/s
        *   8: Axial velocity ranging from roughly -1 to +18 m/s

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   X/R (dimensionless position) ranges roughly from -2.4 to 2.4 (in Figures 9 and 10)
*   Axial velocity ranges roughly from -15 to +35 m/s (Figures 9 and 10).
*   Y/R (dimensionless position) ranges roughly from -1 to 1 (in Figures 7 and 8)
    *   RMSA in Figure 7 varies from 5 m/s to 22 m/s
    *   RMSA in Figure 8 varies from 4 m/s to 12 m/s
*   Curves at Z = 0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.6, 2
